reasoning:
Tan Zhongyi started April at FIDE #3 with a rating of 2555. However, she just lost the April 2025 Women's World Chess Championship match to Ju Wenjun by a wide margin (6.5 to 2.5 over 9 games). Given the 6-point rating difference between them, Tan scored ~1.9 points below her expected score, which at a K-factor of 10 translates to a loss of roughly 19 rating points. This will drop her May 1 FIDE rating to approximately 2536. Consequently, she will fall behind Lei Tingjie (2552) and Aleksandra Goryachkina (2544), moving her down to #5 in the female rankings. 

The Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page updates shortly after FIDE publishes the new official lists on the 1st of each month. By May 4 and 27, it is highly likely that this page will display the May 1 list, meaning her rank will be lower than it was on April 27 (resolving NO). To regain the #3 rank by July or October, she would need to recover the ~16 point gap behind Lei Tingjie, which is difficult but possible over a few months of active play. Uncertainties strictly increase towards 0.5 per instructions.
